outcome_contract.why: The startup loop has no step that requires checking how every screen in the app actually looks when rendered — both in light and dark mode. Issues like invisible text in dark mode, clashing colours, and text that is too small are only caught by doing this audit manually. Adding a screen-by-screen rendered audit to the loop means these problems get found automatically for every product, every time, before launch.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: The startup loop includes a required rendered UI screen audit at S9B that covers every screen in both light and dark mode, with findings saved as an artifact and blocking issues preventing advance.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: This change is to orchestration/config files, not to any rendered UI
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No visual change; the gate enforces visual quality is checked externally

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Operator flow: advance command now blocks if sweep not run or has S1 blockers. Operator must know to run tools-ui-contrast-sweep first.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Operator may be surprised by new block; documentation/next_action message must be clear
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — plan must define the exact block message and next_action string

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No auth, no untrusted input, no data exposure. Gate reads local files only.
Advance command already returns structured packet with gate status. New gate adds fields to this packet.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Gate pass/fail is visible to operator via the advance packet. No separate logging needed.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: No — same packet pattern as GATE-LAUNCH-SEC

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: s6b gate tests establish the pattern. No tests exist for s9b gates.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: New gate logic should have unit tests to prevent regression.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — plan must include test task

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Sweep artifact frontmatter schema (S1-Blockers field). Gate pass/block packet schema.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Frontmatter schema is currently undocumented as a contract. Gate is a new consumer.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — plan must document the frontmatter contract

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Gate is filesystem glob + file read — trivially fast. No hot path risk.
loop-spec.yaml version bump is irreversible (semantic). Gate is additive (new hard gate).
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Existing businesses mid-loop will encounter the new gate at next S9B advance. If no sweep artifact exists, they block. Mitigation: plan must define the grace-period strategy (if any).
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Yes — plan must address existing-run impact

open_operator_questions: Should the new hard gate apply immediately to all businesses, or should there be a 30-day advisory window before it becomes a hard block?

stage_payload.scope_summary: The startup loop's S9B stage currently runs `/lp-launch-qa` (with `/lp-design-qa` as secondary). Neither skill performs a rendered, screen-by-screen visual audit in light and dark mode. `tools-ui-contrast-sweep` — the skill that does exactly this — is documented as sitting at "S9C (Parallel Sweep)" but S9C does not exist in `loop-spec.yaml`. There is no gate enforcing that a contrast-and-uniformity sweep has been run before S9B→SIGNALS advance. This change adds the rendered UI audit requirement to the S9B gate, including a hard gate enforcing that a sweep artifact exists and is unblocked (no u
goals: Add `/tools-ui-contrast-sweep` as a required step in S9B, covering all routes in both light and dark mode.
Add a hard gate `GATE-UI-SWEEP-01` to `s9b-gates.md` that blocks S9B→SIGNALS advance when no recent sweep artifact exists or when the artifact contains unresolved S1 blockers.
Record this gate in `loop-spec.yaml` with a spec version bump.
Update the S9B stage entry in `loop-spec.yaml` to list `tools-ui-contrast-sweep` as a secondary skill alongside `/lp-design-qa`.
Update the startup-loop `SKILL.md` stage map table to reflect the new secondary skill and gate.
non_goals: Redesigning the `/tools-ui-contrast-sweep` workflow — only the artifact schema and the SKILL.md loop-position label require changes as a direct consequence of this gate design.
Adding a new loop stage (S9C as a formal stage ID) — we integrate into the existing S9B stage.
Automating the sweep execution (it remains a skill invocation step; the gate only checks the artifact).
constraints: `loop-spec.yaml` is the runtime-authoritative source. All downstream docs (SKILL.md, workflow guide) must align after a spec version bump (VC-02).
Gate enforcement must remain filesystem-only at advance time (consistent with existing `GATE-LAUNCH-SEC` pattern).
assumptions: The gate checks for the most recent sweep artifact within a 30-day window (consistent with `GATE-LAUNCH-SEC`).
S1 blocker = advance-blocking; S2 and S3 are warning-only at the gate (consistent with the skill's own severity model).
key_entry_points: `.claude/skills/startup-loop/modules/cmd-advance/s9b-gates.md` — gate enforcement module loaded during `startup-loop advance` when current transition is S9B→SIGNALS. This is the canonical location for the new gate.
`.claude/skills/startup-loop/modules/cmd-advance.md` — dispatch table that routes to `s9b-gates.md` (step 6 in the advance module). Will need a line item added for the new gate summary.

`docs/business-os/startup-loop/specifications/loop-spec.yaml` — runtime-authoritative spec. S9B stage definition at line 1214. Requires: secondary skill addition + version comment + spec_version bump.
`.claude/skills/startup-loop/SKILL.md` — stage map table at line 174. Requires update.

analysis_readiness.Recommended next step: `/lp-do-analysis startup-loop-ui-audit-integration` — primary question is the glob scoping approach (Option A / B / C); secondary is whether to add a grace period for existing runs.

rehearsal_issues.Scope Area: Glob scoping approach for artifact discovery
rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: [Scope gap in investigation] Minor: whether artifact slug follows business-nameable pattern is unresolved

process_topology.trigger: `startup-loop advance --business <BIZ>` when current stage is S9B (DO complete, S9B→SIGNALS transition pending).
process_topology.end_condition: SIGNALS stage becomes active; loop operator begins weekly readout.

areas.label: S9B execution
areas.flow: Operator runs `/lp-launch-qa --business <BIZ>` (optionally with `--domain`), then `/lp-design-qa <feature-slug>`. Results are recorded in `launch-qa-report-YYYY-MM-DD.md`.
areas.issues: `/lp-design-qa` is listed as secondary but no gate enforces it ran. No rendered visual audit is required. Dark mode screen issues are invisible to both skills.

areas.label: S9B→SIGNALS advance gate
areas.flow: `GATE-LAUNCH-SEC` (Hard): checks for a launch QA report ≤30 days old with security domain passing. No other gates at this transition.
areas.issues: Only one gate. Visual/UI quality has zero gated enforcement.

areas.label: Contrast/uniformity audit
areas.flow: `/tools-ui-contrast-sweep` exists as a standalone tool. Its own SKILL.md declares "Loop position: S9C (Parallel Sweep)" but S9C is not a stage in `loop-spec.yaml`. It is never invoked by the loop automatically and no gate checks for its artifact.
areas.issues: "S9C" is a stale self-declaration — the loop has no S9C stage. The skill's artifacts are stored in `docs/audits/contrast-sweeps/` but nothing reads them as a gate input.

areas.label: Evidence from recent audit
areas.flow: A full screen audit was run for BRIK reception (2026-03-12) finding 2 S1 blockers, 7 S2 major issues, 5 S3 minor issues — all from a token-level analysis (auth-blocked from live rendering).
areas.issues: Demonstrates real issue class: invisible near-black text on dark shade swatches (~1.5:1 ratio against WCAG 4.5:1 threshold). This would have been missed by both lp-launch-qa and lp-design-qa.
